item.text: I’m treating this pass as “can the tests pass for the wrong reason?” I’m checking the exact execution path behind each new assertion, and I’m expecting to add only the cases that materially harden weak spots like strict-config end-to-end handling and gate-discovery root fallbacks.

I found two places where the code looks solid but the confidence could still be stronger: end-to-end config-load blocking for strict/schema/ENOENT cases, and the “no repo root at all” gate-discovery edge. I’m adding those tests now, plus one cleanup-batch mix case so the regex proves both its negative and positive behavior.

I tightened the weak spots with three additional edge-case tests: no-repo gate discovery, missing-config artifact persistence, strict-unknown-key artifact persistence, and the mixed approved/not-approved cleanup batch. I’m running those touched files first to make sure the new assertions are actually exercising the intended paths.

item.text:
## Findings Fixed (id, summary, files changed)
- `F1` — Added end-to-end blocked-envelope persistence coverage for `ENOENT` and strict-schema rejection in preflight, so `m2` + `m6` now prove both the typed error path and artifact persistence through the real CLI path. Files changed: `processes/impl-cli/tests/preflight-command.test.ts`
- `F2` — Added the no-repo-root gate-discovery edge case so `B1` now proves the filesystem-root walk returns `needs-user-decision` instead of passing incidentally. Files changed: `processes/impl-cli/tests/gate-discovery.test.ts`
- `F3` — Added the mixed approved/not-approved cleanup batch case so `m1` now proves the tightened regex still returns true when a real approved item is present alongside plain-text “not approved” prose. Files changed: `processes/impl-cli/tests/epic-cleanup-command.test.ts`

## Findings Surfaced To Orchestrator (id, summary, why uncertain)
none

## Mock-Shape Audit Per Item (one line each, "real path" | "shallow, fixed in this pass" | "shallow, deferred to orchestrator")
- `B1` — `real path`
- `B2` — `real path`
- `M1` — `real path`
- `M2` — `shallow, fixed in this pass`
- `m1` — `shallow, fixed in this pass`
- `m2` — `real path`
- `m3` — `real path`
- `m4` — `real path`
- `m5` — `real path`
- `m6` — `real path`
- `S1` — `real path`
- `S2` — `real path`
- `S3` — `real path`
- `S4` — `real path`
- `S5` — `real path`

## Edge-Case Coverage Delta (what was added, what was deliberately skipped)
- Added: `B1` no-repo-root edge case in `gate-discovery.test.ts`
- Added: `M2` missing-config-file blocked-envelope persistence in `preflight-command.test.ts`
- Added: `m6 + m2` strict-unknown-key blocked-envelope persistence in `preflight-command.test.ts`
- Added: `m1` mixed approved/plain-text-not-approved positive-match case in `epic-cleanup-command.test.ts`
- Deliberately skipped: garbled `--version` text, because current runtime does not parse version strings semantically; the meaningful routing boundary is success/failure plus auth-tier outcome, which is already covered
- Deliberately skipped: empty-string insert-path variants, because the current loader treats falsy path as absent and unreadable-path behavior is already covered in `security-guardrails.test.ts`
- Spot-checked: `S3` gate-command round-trip still flows into prompt assembly via canonical `bun run green-verify` / `bun run verify-all`, and the existing prompt-assembly assertions still exercise that interpolation path

## Unrelated-Regression Audit Result
No unrelated regression found. The typed-error refactor preserved non-config error classification by routing only `ConfigLoadError` to `INVALID_RUN_CONFIG`; everything else still falls through to `UNEXPECTED_ERROR`. I also re-grepped the command tree and found no remaining `includes("config")` error classification.

## Files Changed In This Pass
- `processes/impl-cli/tests/preflight-command.test.ts`
- `processes/impl-cli/tests/gate-discovery.test.ts`
- `processes/impl-cli/tests/epic-cleanup-command.test.ts`

## Test Count After Pass and Delta
- Total tests after pass: `266`
- Delta from prior baseline `247`: `+19`

## Gate Result After This Pass
`pass` — `bun run capture:test-baseline --force` then `bun run green-verify`

## Recommended Next Step
`ready-for-self-review-3`
